purpose: Identifies whether widely-discussed risks are less likely to be actual catalysts because positioning adjusts, helping distinguish between analytical thoroughness and actionable risk.
components: Concept catalog ratio assessment (bearish:bullish = 25:1 in current KB)
Historical validation: 2008 crisis from invisible risks, not well-known ones
Carry cost asymmetry: 200-300bp/year negative carry for short credit
Distinction between analytical catalog and market positioning
Spread level context: HY OAS 350-420bp vs historical tights
current_assessment: Meta-concern about bearish consensus has merit, but specific evidence (25:1 ratio) conflates research thoroughness with positioning bias. Carry cost is real and quantifiable. Framework valid directionally but current application overstates evidence.
limitations: Cannot distinguish between risks priced in vs merely cataloged
Carry cost calculation assumes static spreads
Cannot identify what the novel catalyst will be
